---
title: "identical twin monitors set up"
date: 2011-09-23
forum: Multimedia Software
---

### Post by briancb on 2011-09-23
Nvidia latest drivers installed, 2 24" BenQ G2420HDB monitors. Nvidia settings show 2 monitors 1/ CRT-0 2/ BenQ G2420HDB. Why it recognises one but not the other, no idea?
When accessing resolution on 1/ top resolution is 1360x768 but on 2/ it is 1920x1200. I have tried changing xorg.conf but end up having just screen  2.

I have both screens working fine but I would like to have a higher resolution.


# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 280.13  (buildmeister@swio-display-x86-rhel47-03.nvidia.com)  Wed Jul 27 17:15:45 PDT 2011

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: xconfig, VertRefresh source: xconfig
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "BenQ"  #I entered this
    ModelName      "G2420HDB"  #I entered this
    HorizSync       28.0 - 55.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce GT 430"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "CRT-0"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT-0: 1360x768 +0+0, CRT-1: 1360x768 +1360+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

---

### Post by wolfen69 on 2011-09-23
Did you try changing the resolution in nvidia-settings, or just through xorg?

---

### Post by briancb on 2011-09-23
Yes, as I said I have both set at 1360x768 that is as high as screen one will go in Nvidia settings Screen 2 will go up to 1920x1200 but having one higher than the other is not what I want.

---

### Post by SecretCode on 2011-09-23
Desktop or laptop? What video card?

---

### Post by briancb on 2011-09-23
Desktop 11.04 Natty 64 bit

GeForce GT 430 

NVIDIA Driver Version: 280.13

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2011-09-23
Re you sure both monitors are connected by VGA.
You can connect both by DVI. (one by single link HDMI)

The GT430 has 1x HDMI, 1x DVI-I & 1xVGA (on full height brkt) ?

Post your /var/log/Xorg.0.log

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2011-09-23
The driver can not read the EDID from CRT-0.
Both monitors are CRT VGA.

The nVidia drivers seem plagued with EDID read problems esp. over VGA.
Or is your VGA cable broken/got pins missing ?

It is easier to fix with DVI than hack the xorg.conf.

You get stuck with 1368x768 because of EDID problem & twinview setup.

Just use DVI for one monitor at least..it is superior to VGA & you could use HDMI audio over DVI (if the monitor allows).
